---
title: "within_condition_binomial_aa"
subtitle: "ribostall stall_sites — enrichment stats"
date: 2026-06-02
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    embed-resources: true
    fig-cap-location: bottom
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        <style>
        table td, table th { padding-top: 0.5em; padding-bottom: 0.5em; }
        </style>
  pdf:
    toc: true
    fig-cap-location: bottom
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        \usepackage{needspace}
        \renewcommand{\arraystretch}{1.5}
tbl-colwidths: [15, 5, 12, 35, 12, 21]
execute:
  echo: false
---

## Composite figure

![BWM 3x3 grid of unweighted amino-acid binomial volcano panels: rows are the three BWM timepoints (top to bottom: Day 0, Day 5, Day 10); columns are ribosome sites in A / P / E order. Each panel plots -log10(FDR) (y) against Log2 Enrichment (x, range ~-1.5 to +1.5); triangles mark FDR<0.05 hits and circles are non-significant. Each glyph is one of the 20 amino acids labelled by its one-letter code and coloured by chemical property: red = acidic, blue = basic, green = hydrophobic, orange = neutral, purple = polar. Horizontal dashed line is FDR = 0.05; vertical dashed lines are at log2 = +/-0.5.](../../plots/within_condition/composite/unweighted/BWM_volcano_grid.png){fig-pos="H" width=92%}

![control 3x3 grid of unweighted amino-acid binomial volcano panels: rows are the three control timepoints (top to bottom: Day 0, Day 5, Day 10); columns are ribosome sites in A / P / E order. Axes, glyph labelling, colour key, and dashed reference lines are identical to the BWM grid above.](../../plots/within_condition/composite/unweighted/control_volcano_grid.png){fig-pos="H" width=92%}

Each glyph is one amino acid tested against its per-group, per-site background frequency (`bg_freq`); a panel is one (group, site) BH-FDR family of 20 amino acids. Positive Log2 Enrichment means the amino acid is over-represented at stall sites relative to its background frequency in that group's transcriptome window; negative means under-represented. The test is **within-group**: BWM and control are not compared directly here — that contrast lives in the per-timepoint Fisher sister file. Panels are arranged in A / P / E ribosome-site order across columns.

---

## Overview

- **CSV:** `within_condition_binomial_aa.csv`
- **Family (from `_INDEX.md`):** `within_condition_binomial`
- **Sister CSV(s):**
  - `within_condition_binomial_codon.csv`
- **Test:** One-sample binomial test (k=`stall_count` out of n=`total_n` vs H0: p=`bg_freq`, two-sided); BH-FDR within each (group, site) family of ~20 amino acids
- **Design:** Per (group, site, aa) one-sample binomial: observed `stall_count` is tested against the binomial null `Binomial(total_n, bg_freq)` where `bg_freq` is the amino-acid frequency in that group's transcriptome window. 6 groups (BWM and control x d0/d5/d10) x 3 sites (A/P/E) x 20 amino acids = 360 tests; replicates are pooled before the test (pseudoreplicated); BH-FDR is applied independently within each of 18 (group, site) families of 20 amino acids. The test is **within-group**: each group is tested against its own background independently and never against another group.

### Family-level caveats

- **pseudorep** — 2 replicates within a group are summed before the binomial test; per-replicate variation is invisible to the statistic, so p-values are anti-conservative.
- **iid-violation-binomial** — the binomial test assumes the `total_n` counted positions are *i.i.d.*, short for **independent and identically distributed**. In plain terms it treats every counted position like a separate, independent coin-flip, each with the same probability (`bg_freq`) of landing on this amino acid. Stall sites break both halves of that assumption. They are not *independent*: one stall on a transcript pulls in neighbouring positions on that same transcript, so many of the counts rise and fall together instead of standing on their own. They are not *identically distributed*: transcripts differ in coverage and sequence, so the per-position probability is not really constant. Because the test believes it has far more independent evidence than it actually does, it reports p-values far smaller (more "certain") than the data warrant. Headline-grade tiny p-values (down to 1e-132 in this file) reflect this broken assumption more than per-feature biological signal — read `log2_enrichment` and `stall_count`, not p magnitude.
- **bg-pseudocount-1e-6** — when a feature is missing from the per-group background, `bg_freq` is floored at 1e-6 (upstream pipeline behaviour); for genuinely absent features this produces extreme `log2_enrichment` when k>0. Mostly an amino-acid-level corner case (does not affect any Top-hits row here, where all `bg_freq` > 0.008).
- **bh-per-(group,site)** — BH-FDR is per (group, site) family of ~20 amino-acid tests. `p_adj` means "corrected within this group at this site," not across the whole CSV.

---

## Headline

Within-group binomial against `bg_freq`, 6 groups (BWM and control x d0/d5/d10) x 3 sites (A/P/E) x 20 amino acids = 360 tests; BH-FDR per (group, site) family of 20. 239/360 hits at FDR<0.05 (264/360 at FDR<0.10). File min `p_adj` = 3.43e-132 at control_day_0 E:K (`log2_enrichment` = +0.625, `stall_count` = 3362, `total_n` = 27732). The dominant structure of this file is **cross-group concordance**: the largest-magnitude cells move in the same direction across all 6 groups, and cells with cross-group direction flips of comparable magnitude do not appear in the data — see the "Cross-group concordance" sub-tables below. p_adj magnitudes down to 1e-132 are co-amplified by `iid-violation-binomial` (within-transcript stall correlation breaking the binomial independence assumption) and by common-amino-acid `bg_freq` tightness (an amino acid with `bg_freq` ~= 0.10 produces extreme p at modest log2 once n exceeds ~20000); magnitude-plus-reproducibility is the anchor for reading.

## Top hits

The effect column is `log2_enrichment` = log2(observed `stall_freq` / `bg_freq`); positive means the amino acid is over-represented at stall sites in that group, negative means under-represented. The three **cross-group** tables below (concordant enrichment, concordant depletion, and discordant cells across the 6 groups) are the file's strongest reproducibility readout and the report's headline sub-tabulation. Each table shows every cell that reached FDR<0.05 in at least 2 of the 6 groups — a reproducibility floor on the `#sig` axis (groups with FDR<0.05, of 6), not a fixed row cap — then ranks the kept cells by `#sig` then `min count`, never by p magnitude: the file's `p-magnitude-anchored-ranking` caveat is the reason: `iid-violation-binomial` and common-amino-acid `bg_freq` tightness inflate p magnitudes for cells with modest effect at large n.

```{=latex}
\needspace{20\baselineskip}
```

Each (site, amino acid) cell pools the six per-group `log2_enrichment` values (the binomial effect vs that group's `bg_freq`). In the `log2_enrichment` column the six values are listed on two lines — **BWM** day_0, day_5, day_10 on the first line, then **control** day_0, day_5, day_10 on the second. **Concordant** cells share one sign across all 6 groups (enrichment = all positive, depletion = all negative); **discordant** cells have at least one sign disagreement across the 6 groups. `#sig` counts the groups with `p_adj` < 0.05 (out of 6); `min count` is the smallest `observed_count` across the 6 groups (the weakest group's data support). Each table shows every cell with `#sig` >= 2 (significant in at least 2 of the 6 groups), displayed sorted by site (A / P / E), then `#sig` desc, then `min count` desc. In the data there are 13 concordant-enriched, 19 concordant-depleted, and 28 discordant cells; after the `#sig` >= 2 floor the tables show 13, 18, and 21 cells respectively (all 13 concordant-enriched cells clear the floor; the 1 concordant-depleted and 7 discordant cells significant in fewer than 2 of the 6 groups are omitted). Amino-acid one-letter codes are spelled out as `<letter> (<full name>)` on every row.

### Concordant enrichment — significant in >= 2 of 6 groups

| Site | amino acid | log2_enrichment | min count | #sig | flag |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| A | E (glutamate) | +0.42, +0.32, +0.54 <br>`\newline`{=latex} +0.35, +0.46, +0.40 | 554 | 6/6 | iid-amp, bg-tight |
| A | D (aspartate) | +0.27, +0.55, +0.54 <br>`\newline`{=latex} +0.31, +0.45, +0.46 | 383 | 6/6 | iid-amp, bg-tight |
| A | Y (tyrosine) | +0.79, +0.22, +0.27 <br>`\newline`{=latex} +0.72, +0.25, +0.60 | 251 | 6/6 | iid-amp |
| A | K (lysine) | +0.18, +0.22, +0.17 <br>`\newline`{=latex} +0.10, +0.31, +0.08 | 675 | 5/6 | iid-amp, bg-tight |
| A | M (methionine) | +0.19, +0.12, +0.36 <br>`\newline`{=latex} +0.20, +0.30, +0.14 | 122 | 3/6 |  |
| P | D (aspartate) | +0.60, +0.57, +0.76 <br>`\newline`{=latex} +0.54, +0.60, +0.73 | 482 | 6/6 | iid-amp, bg-tight |
| P | N (asparagine) | +0.44, +0.48, +0.40 <br>`\newline`{=latex} +0.44, +0.46, +0.53 | 331 | 6/6 | iid-amp |
| P | F (phenylalanine) | +0.21, +0.27, +0.22 <br>`\newline`{=latex} +0.18, +0.34, +0.32 | 254 | 6/6 |  |
| E | K (lysine) | +0.73, +0.55, +0.52 <br>`\newline`{=latex} +0.63, +0.76, +0.58 | 917 | 6/6 | iid-amp, bg-tight |
| E | E (glutamate) | +0.32, +0.32, +0.38 <br>`\newline`{=latex} +0.26, +0.46, +0.41 | 516 | 6/6 | iid-amp, bg-tight |
| E | D (aspartate) | +0.23, +0.18, +0.24 <br>`\newline`{=latex} +0.19, +0.24, +0.16 | 374 | 6/6 | bg-tight |
| E | N (asparagine) | +0.46, +0.41, +0.38 <br>`\newline`{=latex} +0.41, +0.44, +0.49 | 335 | 6/6 | iid-amp |
| E | M (methionine) | +0.25, +0.08, +0.16 <br>`\newline`{=latex} +0.26, +0.27, +0.02 | 127 | 2/6 |  |

: Concordant enrichment — all 6 groups enriched; cells significant in >= 2 of 6 groups, ranked by #sig then min count. {tbl-colwidths="[6,20,37,9,7,21]"}

```{=latex}
\needspace{22\baselineskip}
```

### Concordant depletion — significant in >= 2 of 6 groups

| Site | amino acid | log2_enrichment | min count | #sig | flag |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| A | A (alanine) | -0.69, -0.80, -0.94 <br>`\newline`{=latex} -0.64, -0.77, -0.65 | 289 | 6/6 | iid-amp, bg-tight |
| A | S (serine) | -0.24, -0.32, -0.37 <br>`\newline`{=latex} -0.18, -0.32, -0.37 | 271 | 6/6 | bg-tight |
| A | T (threonine) | -0.57, -0.38, -0.51 <br>`\newline`{=latex} -0.24, -0.37, -0.38 | 217 | 6/6 | bg-tight |
| A | V (valine) | -0.19, -0.14, -0.22 <br>`\newline`{=latex} -0.10, -0.08, -0.18 | 391 | 5/6 | bg-tight |
| A | Q (glutamine) | -0.05, -0.11, -0.37 <br>`\newline`{=latex} -0.11, -0.21, -0.52 | 203 | 4/6 |  |
| P | A (alanine) | -0.81, -0.80, -0.75 <br>`\newline`{=latex} -0.76, -0.62, -0.68 | 294 | 6/6 | iid-amp, bg-tight |
| P | T (threonine) | -0.31, -0.24, -0.38 <br>`\newline`{=latex} -0.09, -0.25, -0.17 | 260 | 6/6 | bg-tight |
| P | S (serine) | -0.47, -0.32, -0.38 <br>`\newline`{=latex} -0.29, -0.28, -0.32 | 232 | 6/6 | iid-amp, bg-tight |
| P | Q (glutamine) | -0.40, -0.33, -0.46 <br>`\newline`{=latex} -0.40, -0.51, -0.65 | 160 | 6/6 | iid-amp |
| P | L (leucine) | -0.18, -0.09, -0.22 <br>`\newline`{=latex} -0.18, -0.18, -0.41 | 411 | 5/6 | iid-amp, bg-tight |
| P | W (tryptophan) | -0.46, -0.46, -0.78 <br>`\newline`{=latex} -0.42, -0.72, -0.96 | 36 | 5/6 | rare-aa (all but ctrl d0) |
| E | A (alanine) | -0.85, -0.77, -0.74 <br>`\newline`{=latex} -0.81, -1.00, -0.76 | 286 | 6/6 | iid-amp, bg-tight |
| E | T (threonine) | -0.38, -0.23, -0.33 <br>`\newline`{=latex} -0.13, -0.48, -0.22 | 248 | 6/6 | iid-amp, bg-tight |
| E | S (serine) | -0.58, -0.18, -0.31 <br>`\newline`{=latex} -0.31, -0.25, -0.26 | 215 | 6/6 | iid-amp, bg-tight |
| E | C (cysteine) | -1.13, -0.93, -0.50 <br>`\newline`{=latex} -0.96, -0.88, -0.81 | 34 | 6/6 | iid-amp, rare-aa (all but ctrl d0) |
| E | L (leucine) | -0.24, -0.12, -0.11 <br>`\newline`{=latex} -0.23, -0.25, -0.13 | 392 | 5/6 | iid-amp, bg-tight |
| E | Y (tyrosine) | -0.42, -0.19, -0.38 <br>`\newline`{=latex} -0.10, -0.43, -0.46 | 128 | 5/6 |  |
| E | F (phenylalanine) | -0.33, -0.09, -0.13 <br>`\newline`{=latex} -0.16, -0.27, -0.19 | 174 | 4/6 |  |

: Concordant depletion — all 6 groups depleted; cells significant in >= 2 of 6 groups, ranked by #sig then min count. {tbl-colwidths="[6,20,37,9,7,21]"}

```{=latex}
\needspace{22\baselineskip}
```

### Discordant — significant in >= 2 of 6 groups

| Site | amino acid | log2_enrichment | min count | #sig | flag |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| A | N (asparagine) | -0.86, -0.16, -0.19 <br>`\newline`{=latex} -0.22, +0.06, -0.40 | 134 | 5/6 | iid-amp |
| A | G (glycine) | +0.18, +0.04, +0.40 <br>`\newline`{=latex} -0.17, +0.03, +0.25 | 536 | 4/6 | iid-amp, bg-tight |
| A | I (isoleucine) | -0.08, +0.15, +0.18 <br>`\newline`{=latex} +0.06, +0.30, +0.17 | 326 | 4/6 | bg-tight |
| A | R (arginine) | +0.10, +0.14, +0.18 <br>`\newline`{=latex} +0.05, -0.02, +0.18 | 442 | 3/6 | bg-tight |
| A | L (leucine) | -0.02, -0.07, -0.42 <br>`\newline`{=latex} +0.04, -0.32, -0.28 | 390 | 3/6 | bg-tight |
| A | P (proline) | +0.15, -0.01, -0.11 <br>`\newline`{=latex} -0.09, -0.21, +0.14 | 263 | 2/6 |  |
| A | W (tryptophan) | +0.77, +0.02, +0.33 <br>`\newline`{=latex} +0.60, -0.04, +0.16 | 78 | 2/6 | iid-amp, rare-aa (BWM d0, BWM d10, ctrl d5, ctrl d10) |
| A | C (cysteine) | +0.01, -0.29, -0.23 <br>`\newline`{=latex} -0.05, -0.31, +0.19 | 75 | 2/6 | rare-aa (BWM d0, BWM d10) |
| P | E (glutamate) | -0.01, +0.10, +0.36 <br>`\newline`{=latex} +0.08, +0.20, +0.38 | 412 | 5/6 | iid-amp, bg-tight |
| P | G (glycine) | +0.24, +0.10, +0.44 <br>`\newline`{=latex} -0.03, +0.13, +0.39 | 559 | 4/6 | iid-amp, bg-tight |
| P | I (isoleucine) | +0.17, +0.21, +0.08 <br>`\newline`{=latex} +0.20, +0.27, -0.10 | 387 | 4/6 | bg-tight |
| P | K (lysine) | +0.17, +0.06, -0.10 <br>`\newline`{=latex} +0.16, -0.10, -0.25 | 595 | 3/6 | bg-tight |
| P | Y (tyrosine) | +0.16, +0.17, -0.00 <br>`\newline`{=latex} +0.33, +0.05, +0.29 | 192 | 3/6 | iid-amp |
| P | V (valine) | -0.13, -0.10, -0.02 <br>`\newline`{=latex} -0.09, +0.08, -0.20 | 409 | 2/6 | bg-tight |
| P | M (methionine) | +0.23, -0.05, -0.08 <br>`\newline`{=latex} +0.14, -0.17, -0.38 | 114 | 2/6 |  |
| P | C (cysteine) | -0.22, -0.05, +0.01 <br>`\newline`{=latex} -0.20, -0.09, +0.28 | 64 | 2/6 | rare-aa (BWM d0, BWM d10) |
| E | R (arginine) | +0.07, -0.16, -0.06 <br>`\newline`{=latex} -0.08, -0.10, -0.29 | 431 | 3/6 | bg-tight |
| E | V (valine) | -0.14, -0.10, -0.14 <br>`\newline`{=latex} +0.02, -0.19, -0.15 | 407 | 3/6 | bg-tight |
| E | Q (glutamine) | -0.13, +0.14, +0.11 <br>`\newline`{=latex} +0.06, +0.21, +0.17 | 192 | 3/6 |  |
| E | G (glycine) | -0.07, -0.20, +0.14 <br>`\newline`{=latex} -0.26, +0.01, -0.03 | 450 | 2/6 | iid-amp, bg-tight |
| E | I (isoleucine) | +0.07, +0.18, -0.14 <br>`\newline`{=latex} +0.17, +0.01, +0.11 | 361 | 2/6 | bg-tight |

: Discordant — at least one sign disagreement across the 6 groups; cells significant in >= 2 of 6 groups, ranked by #sig then min count. {tbl-colwidths="[6,20,37,9,7,21]"}

### Flag glossary

- `iid-amp` — short for "**i.i.d.-violation amplified**" (i.i.d. = independent and identically distributed; see the `iid-violation-binomial` caveat under Family-level caveats for the full plain-language explanation). Applied to every Top-hits row with `p_adj` < 1e-10. The short version: the binomial test assumes every counted position is an independent coin-flip, but stall positions on the same transcript move together — so the test acts as if it has far more independent evidence than it does, and even a modest enrichment gets an astronomically tiny p. The flag is a reminder that this row's p looks tiny because of the broken independence assumption, not because the effect is biologically large. Read it by `log2_enrichment` + reproducibility, never by how small the p is.
- `bg-tight` — short for "**background-frequency tight**," meaning a narrow null. Applied to rows for a common amino acid (`bg_freq` > 0.05). For a common residue counted over tens of thousands of positions, the range of counts the binomial expects purely by chance (its null distribution) is very narrow, so even a small departure from `bg_freq` lands far out in the tail and produces a tiny p. Same upshot as `iid-amp`: the small p reflects how sharp the null is at large n, not a large biological effect. The two flags co-occur on common-amino-acid rows with extreme p.
- `rare-aa` — at least one of the 6 groups has `observed_count` below the stability threshold (k<100). The flag names the low-count groups — it lists them (e.g. `rare-aa (BWM d0)`), collapses to `(all but <group>)` when only one group clears the threshold, and to `(all)` when none do. Direction is reliable; magnitude is not. The rare-aa rows in the tables above are E:C (cysteine) and P:W (tryptophan) in the concordant-depletion table and A:W, A:C, P:C in the discordant table; Cys and Trp are single-resolution corner cases the codon sister file does not rescue (Trp is the single-codon TGG).

## Numbers at a glance

- `n_tests`: 360 (6 groups x 3 sites x 20 amino acids)
- `n_significant` (adjusted-p < 0.05): 239
- `n_significant` (adjusted-p < 0.10): 264
- `min adjusted-p`: 3.43e-132 (control_day_0, E site, K; log2=+0.625, k=3362/27732) — under `iid-violation-binomial` and `bg-tight`, this row's flag column carries both flags; this is **not** a magnitude claim.
- `p_floor`: n/a — no exact-test floor for the binomial at these n.
- Per (group, site) BH families (each is 20 amino-acid tests); each cell shows hits at FDR<0.05 (of 20), the smallest p_adj in that family, and the amino acid that achieves it (one-letter code):

| group | A site | P site | E site |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| BWM_day_0 | 12/20; 1.54e-20 (A) | 12/20; 7.63e-27 (A) | 12/20; 8.70e-53 (K) |
| BWM_day_5 | 12/20; 1.43e-48 (A) | 11/20; 2.42e-49 (A) | 15/20; 7.69e-48 (K) |
| BWM_day_10 | 15/20; 3.26e-36 (A) | 12/20; 2.65e-32 (D) | 9/20; 1.09e-26 (K) |
| control_day_0 | 14/20; 3.98e-84 (A) | 17/20; 2.50e-111 (A) | 14/20; 3.43e-132 (K) |
| control_day_5 | 13/20; 1.69e-41 (A) | 12/20; 2.69e-33 (D) | 15/20; 4.82e-90 (K) |
| control_day_10 | 14/20; 1.36e-27 (A) | 16/20; 6.53e-43 (D) | 14/20; 1.30e-41 (K) |

: Per (group, site) BH family of 20 amino acids: hits at FDR<0.05, min p_adj, and the amino acid achieving it. {tbl-colwidths="[22,26,26,26]"}

## Biological interpretation

**The dominant signal of this file is cross-group concordance, not perturbation (concrete).** The largest-magnitude cells (mean `|log2_enrichment|` > 0.4) all move in the same direction across BWM and control at d0, d5, and d10. Of the 60 (site, amino acid) cells, 13 are concordantly enriched and 19 concordantly depleted across all 6 groups, leaving 28 with any cross-group sign disagreement (the partition feeding the three Top-hits tables, each filtered to cells significant in >= 2 of the 6 groups); the largest-magnitude cells do not flip direction across the 6 groups. The per-(group, site) counts of FDR<0.05 cells are broadly similar across the six groups (9/20 to 17/20; Numbers at a glance), with no group's amino-acid map standing apart — and because the test is within-group against each group's own background, these counts are not a BWM-vs-control contrast in any case. The file therefore reads as a structural map of which amino acids are over- and under-represented at each ribosome site relative to their transcriptome-window frequency, stable across perturbation and time. The biology this report can extract from this CSV is "what does the stalling-prone residue map look like" — perturbation contrasts live in the per-timepoint Fisher sister file.

**E-site lysine and P-site aspartate are the two strongest reproducible enrichments (concrete).** Lys at the E site has mean `log2_enrichment` +0.63 (range +0.52 to +0.76 across the 6 groups, `stall_count` 917-3362) and Asp at the P site has mean +0.63 (range +0.54 to +0.76, `stall_count` 482-2344). E-site lysine is a textbook ribosome-pause anchor: as the ribosome translocates, the positively charged lysine side-chain continues to interact electrostatically with the negatively charged exit tunnel, slowing the elongation cycle. P-site aspartate is consistent with peptide-bond chemistry that pauses on acidic residues: aspartate is concordantly enriched at all three sites (A, P, E; FDR<0.05 in every group), and glutamate is concordantly enriched at the A and E sites (means +0.42 and +0.36) while at the P site it is weaker and falls in the discordant set, so the P-site acidic signal is carried mainly by aspartate. Together the acidic residues reinforce the acidic-residue pause-promoting reading. The stability across BWM/control x d0/d5/d10 is what makes these concordance signals rather than perturbation signals.

**Small-residue depletion (alanine, serine, threonine) across all three sites is the structural counterpart to the enrichment story (concrete).** Alanine at site E (mean -0.82), site A (mean -0.75), and site P (mean -0.74) is depleted in every group with `stall_count` >= 286 in every group. Alanine has the smallest non-glycine side-chain among the proteinogenic amino acids and is mechanistically the most pause-resistant residue: it interacts weakly with the exit tunnel and decodes via abundant tRNA-Ala isoacceptors. Serine and threonine, the two small hydroxyl-bearing residues, are concordantly depleted at all three sites alongside it (serine site means ~-0.3, threonine ~-0.3 to -0.4, FDR<0.05 in all 6 groups, `min count` >= 215 so neither is rare-flagged), so the depleted side is a coherent small / short-side-chain class rather than alanine alone. The uniform under-representation at stall sites mirrors the K/D/E enrichment story by inversion — stalling concentrates on residues with pause-promoting side-chain properties (positively charged, acidic, aromatic) and avoids the small, fast-decoding residues least likely to pause.

**Rare-amino-acid cells reach large `|log2_enrichment|` but are flagged for ranking discipline (exploratory).** Cys at the E site (`stall_count` 34-191 across groups, `|log2_enrichment|` up to 1.13 in BWM_day_0) is concordantly depleted in all 6 groups, and Trp at the P site (`stall_count` 36-263, `|log2_enrichment|` up to 0.96 in control_day_10) is concordantly depleted with `#sig` 5/6; both appear in the concordant-depletion table carrying the `rare-aa` flag. Both reach magnitudes comparable to the concordance cells, but the per-CSV `rare-aa-low-count` caveat flags them: their log2 estimates are noisier than common-amino-acid cells (where `stall_count` typically runs 300-3000), so their magnitude reflects low-count accident as much as biology. Direction is the usable signal for these rows; magnitude is not. Trp at P site is a single-codon amino acid (TGG), so the rare flag persists at codon resolution in the sister CSV — no synonym-resolution rescue is possible.

**The headline-grade p magnitudes (down to 1e-132) are iid-violation amplification, not biological strength (concrete).** The file-minimum `p_adj` cell is control_day_0 E:K at 3.43e-132 — a p-value that is not interpretable on the usual "smaller p = stronger biology" axis. The `iid-violation-binomial` caveat names the structural reason: the binomial null assumes 27732 iid Bernoulli draws (one per stall-site position) with p=`bg_freq`, but stall events on the same transcript are not independent (one stall site biases neighbouring positions; per-transcript coverage varies). Combined with the common-amino-acid `bg_freq` tightness (Lys `bg_freq` ~= 0.08, so the binomial null is sharp), modest enrichments at very large n compress to vanishing p-values. Read this file by `log2_enrichment` magnitude and cross-group reproducibility, in that order; treat any `p_adj` < 1e-10 as flagged by `iid-amp` (and `bg-tight` for high-`bg_freq` cells) regardless of the magnitude.

**What to carry forward.** *Concrete*: the cross-group concordance set (13 concordantly enriched + 19 concordantly depleted of the 60 (site, amino acid) cells) as a structural map of stall-site amino-acid composition that is stable across perturbation and time; E:K and P:D as the strongest enriched cells; uniform small-residue depletion (alanine, serine, threonine) at A/P/E as the structural-baseline counterpart; acidic residues (D, E) over-represented at A and P sites alongside the headline cells. *Exploratory*: rare-amino-acid cells (E:C, P:W) — direction reliable, magnitude not, worth checking the codon sister CSV for synonym distribution (Trp is single-codon, so the rare flag persists at codon resolution). *Noise*: any per-cell p-magnitude reading above the iid-amplification floor — quote magnitude + reproducibility, not p. Cross-checks worth running in sister files: does the per-timepoint Fisher show BWM-vs-control divergence on top of any of these concordance cells (perturbation overlay on the structural map); does the within-condition binomial codon sister split any of the amino-acid-level concordance into a single-synonym story (refining the biological reading from "amino-acid property" to "specific tRNA/codon").

## Methods

The test is a one-sample binomial (`stall_count` ~ Binomial(`total_n`, `bg_freq`), two-sided), with BH-FDR computed within each (group, site) family of 20 amino-acid tests (each of the 18 sub-families is corrected independently, not against the full 360). Effect column is `log2_enrichment` = log2(`stall_freq` / `bg_freq`); a count-weighted variant `weighted_log2_enrichment` is also present in the CSV but is not used for ranking here (the unweighted log2 is what the binomial p reflects). The test answers "is amino acid X observed at stall sites at a different frequency than its `bg_freq` in the same group's transcriptome window?", **not** "is amino acid X enriched at BWM stall sites relative to control" (that is the per-timepoint Fisher) and **not** "does the within-condition replicate-level frequency change between timepoints" (that is between-timepoint Wilcoxon). Counts (`stall_count`) and totals (`total_n`) are summed across replicates before the test, hence the family-level pseudoreplication caveat. Top hits are presented as three cross-group tables that partition the 60 (site, amino acid) cells by the sign of `log2_enrichment` across the 6 groups: concordant enrichment (positive in all 6 groups; 13 cells), concordant depletion (negative in all 6; 19 cells), and discordant (at least one sign disagreement; 28 cells). A cell enters the partition only if it has a value in all 6 groups. Each table shows every cell that reached `p_adj` < 0.05 in at least 2 of the 6 groups (`#sig` >= 2, a reproducibility floor on the `#sig` axis rather than a fixed row cap), the kept cells ranked by `#sig` descending then `min count` (the smallest `observed_count` across the 6 groups) descending, then displayed in A / P / E site order; the three tables show 13, 18, and 21 cells respectively. This cross-group view is the lead Top-hits tabulation because the file's dominant structure is reproducibility across groups rather than per-group novelty, and ranking by p magnitude is deliberately avoided since `iid-violation-binomial` and common-amino-acid `bg_freq` tightness inflate p for modest effects at large n.

## What the caveats mean for reading this file

- **pseudorep.** The binomial test pools replicates into one `stall_count` and one `total_n` per (group, site, aa) cell; per-replicate variation is invisible to the statistic, so p-values are anti-conservative — treat the smallest p-magnitudes as inflated, not as additional certainty.
- **iid-violation-binomial.** The binomial null assumes the `total_n` counted positions are i.i.d. (independent and identically distributed — see Family-level caveats), but stall events on the same transcript are correlated and transcripts vary in coverage; this is the dominant reason p-values compress to 1e-22 and beyond. Rank by `log2_enrichment` + `stall_count` + cross-group reproducibility, not by p magnitude.
- **bg-pseudocount-1e-6.** The upstream pipeline floors `bg_freq` at 1e-6 to avoid log2 division-by-zero; does not affect any Top-hits row (all have `bg_freq` > 0.008). Only relevant for genuinely absent features, which produce extreme `log2_enrichment` when k>0.
- **bh-per-(group, site).** BH-FDR is per (group, site) family of 20; site-level FDR control is per-20-aa, not pooled across the 60 amino acids per group. Two rows with the same raw p in different (group, site) families can land at different `p_adj`.
- **rare-aa-low-count.** Cells with `stall_count` below ~100 in some groups (lowest: BWM_d10 P:W k=36; BWM_d0 E:C k=34) have noisier log2 estimates and carry the `rare-aa` flag where displayed. Under the `#sig` >= 2 floor both clear the threshold and appear in the concordant-depletion table: E:C (`min count` 34, `#sig` 6/6, `|log2_enrichment|` up to 1.13 in BWM_day_0) and P:W (`min count` 36, `#sig` 5/6, `|log2_enrichment|` up to ~0.96 in control_day_10); the discordant table likewise carries rare-aa A:W, A:C, and P:C. The tables sort by `#sig` then `min count`, so these low-count cells sit toward the bottom within their site block. Read direction, not magnitude, for these rows; do not rank them alongside high-count cells.
- **small-bh-family-discreteness.** Each (group, site) BH family is only 20 tests, but with the `iid-violation-binomial`-driven extreme p magnitudes, discreteness rarely binds (per-family FDR<0.05 hit fractions run 9/20 to 17/20). Flag carried for parity with the sister codon CSV, where larger 61-codon BH families behave differently.
- **p-magnitude-anchored-ranking.** `p_adj` down to 3.4e-132 is best read as a joint readout of (`log2_enrichment` magnitude) x (`total_n`) x (`iid-violation-binomial` amplification factor), not as biological strength. The Top hits flag column applies `iid-amp` to every `p_adj` < 1e-10 row regardless of direction (no selective dampening), and adds `bg-tight` for rows with `bg_freq` > 0.05 (which makes the binomial null especially tight at high n). Reading priority: `log2_enrichment` magnitude + `stall_count` + cross-group reproducibility, in that order.

### Considered and ruled out

*(none — no caveats were considered-but-not-applicable for this file.)*

---

## Plots — individual

Each panel below is one (group, site) BH-FDR family of 20 amino acids, plotted as a volcano: `log2_enrichment` (x-range ~+/-1.5) on the x-axis, -log10(FDR) on the y-axis. Triangles mark FDR<0.05; circles are non-significant. Amino acids are labelled by one-letter code and coloured by chemical property (red = acidic, blue = basic, green = hydrophobic, orange = neutral, purple = polar). Horizontal dashed line marks FDR = 0.05; vertical dashed lines mark log2 = +/-0.5.

![BWM Day 0 A-site unweighted volcano. A (Ala, hydrophobic green) at log2 ~= -0.69, -log10(FDR) ~= 20 sits as the largest depleted hit (top-left). Y (Tyr, hydrophobic green) at log2 ~= +0.79, -log10(FDR) ~= 17 is the largest enriched hit. N (Asn, polar purple) at log2 ~= -0.84, -log10(FDR) ~= 13 and E (Glu, acidic red) at log2 ~= +0.40, -log10(FDR) ~= 10 are next-largest. T (Thr, polar) and W (Trp, hydrophobic) are also FDR<0.05.](../../plots/within_condition/individual/unweighted/BWM_day_0_A_volcano.png)

![BWM Day 0 P-site unweighted volcano. A (Ala, hydrophobic green) at log2 ~= -0.81, -log10(FDR) ~= 26 sits as the most extreme depleted hit (top-left, panel-maximum y). D (Asp, acidic red) at log2 ~= +0.60, -log10(FDR) ~= 17 is the largest enriched hit. N (Asn, polar purple), S (Ser, polar), and G (Gly, neutral orange) are next; K (Lys, basic blue) and T (Thr, polar) cross FDR<0.05 at modest magnitudes.](../../plots/within_condition/individual/unweighted/BWM_day_0_P_volcano.png)

![BWM Day 0 E-site unweighted volcano. K (Lys, basic blue) at log2 ~= +0.73, -log10(FDR) ~= 53 sits as the panel-maximum enriched hit. A (Ala, hydrophobic green) at log2 ~= -0.85, -log10(FDR) ~= 29 is the largest depleted hit. C (Cys, hydrophobic green) at log2 ~= -1.13, -log10(FDR) ~= 6 is rare-aa-flagged. S (Ser, polar), N (Asn, polar), and E (Glu, acidic red) also clear FDR<0.05.](../../plots/within_condition/individual/unweighted/BWM_day_0_E_volcano.png)

![BWM Day 5 A-site unweighted volcano. A (Ala, hydrophobic green) at log2 ~= -0.80, -log10(FDR) ~= 48 sits as the largest depleted hit. D (Asp, acidic red) at log2 ~= +0.55, -log10(FDR) ~= 29 and E (Glu, acidic red) at log2 ~= +0.40, -log10(FDR) ~= 7 are the largest enriched hits. K (Lys, basic blue) and T (Thr, polar) also FDR<0.05.](../../plots/within_condition/individual/unweighted/BWM_day_5_A_volcano.png)

![BWM Day 5 P-site unweighted volcano. A (Ala, hydrophobic green) at log2 ~= -0.80, -log10(FDR) ~= 49 sits as the panel-maximum depleted hit. D (Asp, acidic red) at log2 ~= +0.57, -log10(FDR) ~= 32 and N (Asn, polar purple) at log2 ~= +0.48, -log10(FDR) ~= 17 are the largest enriched hits. W (Trp, hydrophobic green) at log2 ~= -0.46, -log10(FDR) ~= 2 is rare-aa-flagged.](../../plots/within_condition/individual/unweighted/BWM_day_5_P_volcano.png)

![BWM Day 5 E-site unweighted volcano. K (Lys, basic blue) at log2 ~= +0.55, -log10(FDR) ~= 47 and A (Ala, hydrophobic green) at log2 ~= -0.77, -log10(FDR) ~= 46 sit as the panel's enriched and depleted maxima. N (Asn, polar purple), D (Asp, acidic red), and E (Glu, acidic red) are next-largest on the enriched side; C (Cys, hydrophobic green, rare-aa) is the largest non-A depleted hit.](../../plots/within_condition/individual/unweighted/BWM_day_5_E_volcano.png)

![BWM Day 10 A-site unweighted volcano. A (Ala, hydrophobic green) at log2 ~= -0.94, -log10(FDR) ~= 35 sits as the largest depleted hit. E (Glu, acidic red) at log2 ~= +0.54, -log10(FDR) ~= 21 and D (Asp, acidic red) at log2 ~= +0.54, -log10(FDR) ~= 15 are the largest enriched hits; T (Thr, polar) and G (Gly, neutral orange) also FDR<0.05.](../../plots/within_condition/individual/unweighted/BWM_day_10_A_volcano.png)

![BWM Day 10 P-site unweighted volcano. A (Ala, hydrophobic green) at log2 ~= -0.75, -log10(FDR) ~= 25 sits as the largest depleted hit; D (Asp, acidic red) at log2 ~= +0.76, -log10(FDR) ~= 32 is the largest enriched hit (panel-max). G (Gly, neutral orange) at log2 ~= +0.44, -log10(FDR) ~= 14, N (Asn, polar purple), and W (Trp, hydrophobic green, rare-aa-flagged) round out FDR<0.05.](../../plots/within_condition/individual/unweighted/BWM_day_10_P_volcano.png)

![BWM Day 10 E-site unweighted volcano. K (Lys, basic blue) at log2 ~= +0.52, -log10(FDR) ~= 26 sits as the largest enriched hit. A (Ala, hydrophobic green) at log2 ~= -0.74, -log10(FDR) ~= 25 is the largest depleted hit. E (Glu, acidic red), N (Asn, polar purple), D (Asp, acidic red), and T (Thr, polar) round out the FDR<0.05 hits. Fewest E-site BWM hits in the file (9/20).](../../plots/within_condition/individual/unweighted/BWM_day_10_E_volcano.png)

![control Day 0 A-site unweighted volcano. Y (Tyr, hydrophobic green) at log2 ~= +0.72, -log10(FDR) ~= 64 sits as the panel-maximum enriched hit. A (Ala, hydrophobic green) at log2 ~= -0.64, -log10(FDR) ~= 84 is the largest depleted hit (and one of the file's largest control_day_0 depletions). W (Trp, hydrophobic green) at log2 ~= +0.60 is enriched, E (Glu, acidic red), and D (Asp, acidic red) also FDR<0.05.](../../plots/within_condition/individual/unweighted/control_day_0_A_volcano.png)

![control Day 0 P-site unweighted volcano. A (Ala, hydrophobic green) at log2 ~= -0.76, -log10(FDR) ~= 111 is the largest depleted hit and the panel-maximum y. D (Asp, acidic red) at log2 ~= +0.55, -log10(FDR) ~= 70 and N (Asn, polar purple) at log2 ~= +0.44, -log10(FDR) ~= 32 are the largest enriched hits. Q (Gln, polar purple, depleted) and W (Trp, hydrophobic green, depleted, rare-aa) also FDR<0.05; densest P-site panel in the file (17/20 hits).](../../plots/within_condition/individual/unweighted/control_day_0_P_volcano.png)

![control Day 0 E-site unweighted volcano. K (Lys, basic blue) at log2 ~= +0.63, -log10(FDR) ~= 132 sits as the file-minimum `p_adj` cell — visibly the most extreme point in the entire dataset. A (Ala, hydrophobic green) at log2 ~= -0.81, -log10(FDR) ~= 123 is the largest depleted hit. C (Cys, hydrophobic green, rare-aa), N (Asn, polar purple), and E (Glu, acidic red) also FDR<0.05.](../../plots/within_condition/individual/unweighted/control_day_0_E_volcano.png)

![control Day 5 A-site unweighted volcano. E (Glu, acidic red) at log2 ~= +0.46, -log10(FDR) ~= 26 is the largest enriched hit. A (Ala, hydrophobic green) at log2 ~= -0.77, -log10(FDR) ~= 41 is the largest depleted hit. Y (Tyr, hydrophobic green), L (Leu, hydrophobic green), T (Thr, polar), and N (Asn, polar purple) also FDR<0.05.](../../plots/within_condition/individual/unweighted/control_day_5_A_volcano.png)

![control Day 5 P-site unweighted volcano. A (Ala, hydrophobic green) at log2 ~= -0.62, -log10(FDR) ~= 30 sits as the largest depleted hit. D (Asp, acidic red) at log2 ~= +0.60, -log10(FDR) ~= 33 and N (Asn, polar purple) at log2 ~= +0.46, -log10(FDR) ~= 15 are the largest enriched hits. W (Trp, hydrophobic green) and Q (Gln, polar) also FDR<0.05.](../../plots/within_condition/individual/unweighted/control_day_5_P_volcano.png)

![control Day 5 E-site unweighted volcano. K (Lys, basic blue) at log2 ~= +0.76, -log10(FDR) ~= 90 is the panel-maximum enriched hit. A (Ala, hydrophobic green) at log2 ~= -1.00, -log10(FDR) ~= 63 is the largest depleted hit (largest control_day_5 depletion magnitude). E (Glu, acidic red), C (Cys, hydrophobic green, rare-aa), T (Thr, polar) also FDR<0.05.](../../plots/within_condition/individual/unweighted/control_day_5_E_volcano.png)

![control Day 10 A-site unweighted volcano. Y (Tyr, hydrophobic green) at log2 ~= +0.60, -log10(FDR) ~= 13 is the largest enriched hit. A (Ala, hydrophobic green) at log2 ~= -0.65, -log10(FDR) ~= 27 is the largest depleted hit. E (Glu, acidic red), D (Asp, acidic red), L (Leu, hydrophobic green) also FDR<0.05.](../../plots/within_condition/individual/unweighted/control_day_10_A_volcano.png)

![control Day 10 P-site unweighted volcano. A (Ala, hydrophobic green) at log2 ~= -0.68, -log10(FDR) ~= 30 sits as the largest depleted hit. D (Asp, acidic red) at log2 ~= +0.74, -log10(FDR) ~= 43 and N (Asn, polar purple) at log2 ~= +0.53, -log10(FDR) ~= 15 are the largest enriched hits. W (Trp, hydrophobic green, rare-aa), G (Gly, neutral orange), Q (Gln, polar) also FDR<0.05 (16/20 hits at this P site).](../../plots/within_condition/individual/unweighted/control_day_10_P_volcano.png)

![control Day 10 E-site unweighted volcano. K (Lys, basic blue) at log2 ~= +0.58, -log10(FDR) ~= 42 is the panel-maximum enriched hit. A (Ala, hydrophobic green) at log2 ~= -0.76, -log10(FDR) ~= 36 is the largest depleted hit. N (Asn, polar purple) at log2 ~= +0.49 enriched, E (Glu, acidic red) enriched; C (Cys, hydrophobic green, rare-aa) and W (Trp, hydrophobic green, rare-aa) also FDR<0.05.](../../plots/within_condition/individual/unweighted/control_day_10_E_volcano.png)
